perturbation.original_text: The term "Evaluation Material" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives, (ii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the source of such information was not known by the receiving party to the bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the receiving party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing party or any of its Representatives, provided that such source was not known by the receiving party to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information, (iv) is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a duty of confidentiality, (v) is independently developed by the receiving party or any of its Representatives without use of Evaluation Material and can be proved by competent evidence, (vi) is disclosed under operation of applicable law or regulation, or (vii) is disclosed by the receiving party or its Representatives with the discloser's prior written approval.
perturbation.changed_text: The term "Evaluation Material" does not include information which (i) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Personnel, (ii) was within the receiving party's possession prior to its being furnished to the receiving party by or on behalf of the disclosing party, provided that the source of such information was not known by the receiving party to the bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information, (iii) is or becomes available to the receiving party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than the disclosing party or any of its Personnel, provided that such source was not known by the receiving party to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other contractual, legal or fiduciary obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing party or any other party with respect to such information, (iv) is disclosed by the disclosing party to a third party without a duty of confidentiality, (v) is independently developed by the receiving party or any of its Representatives without use of Evaluation Material and can be proved by competent evidence, (vi) is disclosed under operation of applicable law or regulation, or (vii) is disclosed by the receiving party or its Representatives with the discloser's prior written approval.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Representatives' is replaced by 'Personnel' in (i) and (iii) to create an in-text contradiction. This means that the breach of agreement, by the receiving party, 'Representatives', or the source of confidential information now depends on whether the term can be 'Personnel' or 'Representatives', since now they are not the same. This change causes uncertainty as to the scope of exceptions of what constitutes 'Evaluation Material' because 'Personnel' has a different meaning than 'Representatives', which is contradicting on the definition of Evaluation Material on (1).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Evaluation Material. The term "Evaluation Material" also shall be deemed to include all notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents (whether in paper, electronic or any other form) prepared by each or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished to such party or its Representatives pursuant hereto which is not available to the general public and would otherwise constitute Evaluation Material hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Representatives' with 'Personnel' in the definition of 'Evaluation Material'. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Evaluation Material' in relation to actions of 'Representatives'. By changing 'Representatives' to 'Personnel' in the exceptions to the definition, the modified text creates a contradiction. The definition of Evaluation material depends on which term is used (personnel or representatives) which triggers a contradiction because the terms are not identical.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the other's Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of evaluating a possible Transaction between the parties and, if the parties determine to proceed with a Transaction, the negotiation, execution and performance of a definitive agreement with respect thereto, and that the disclosing party's Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and each party and its Representatives will not disclose or use for purposes other than the purposes stated above any of the other's Evaluation Material in any other manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the receiving party may make any disclosure of such information to which the disclosing party gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed to the receiving party's Representatives which need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction between the parties, who are informed of the confidential nature of such information and are subject to confidentiality and non-use obligations at least as restrictive as defined herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereby agrees that it and its Affiliates shall use the other's Evaluation Material solely for the purposes of evaluating a possible Transaction between the parties and, if the parties determine to proceed with a Transaction, the negotiation, execution and performance of a definitive agreement with respect thereto, and that the disclosing party's Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and each party and its Affiliates will not disclose or use for purposes other than the purposes stated above any of the other's Evaluation Material in any other manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) the receiving party may make any disclosure of such information to which the disclosing party gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed to the receiving party's Representatives which need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction between the parties, who are informed of the confidential nature of such information and are subject to confidentiality and non-use obligations at least as restrictive as defined herein.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Representatives' is changed to 'Affiliates' in the main part of the sentence, but not in the exception (ii) part, which mentions 'Representatives'. This creates a contradiction: Can the information be disclosed to 'Representatives'? 'Affiliates' is an undefined term here. This contradicts the initial definition of who can see the information described in section (1)
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to such information being furnished to each party and its directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) (collectively, "Representatives"), each party agrees to treat any nonpublic information concerning to other party (whether prepared by the disclosing party, its advisors or otherwise and irrespective of the form of communication) which is furnished hereunder to a party or to its Representatives now or in the future by or on behalf of the disclosing party (herein collectively referred to as the "Evaluation Material") in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Representatives' with 'Affiliates' in the main clause regarding the use of Evaluation Material, but keeps 'Representatives' in the exception clause (ii). This creates a contradiction because the contract now implies that Evaluation Material can be disclosed to 'Representatives' under the exception, while the main clause, as modified, only mentions 'Affiliates'. The contradicted text defines 'Representatives', creating a direct conflict over to whom the information can be disclosed. The change creates ambiguity and contradicts the initial definition of who can see the information, leading to uncertainty and potential enforceability issues, as per the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Employees.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Representatives' is changed to 'Employees'. This creates a contradiction because 'Representatives' is defined to include other entities apart from the employee, so the legal responsability is now limited to the employees, leading to uncertainty. It directly contradicts to the initial definition of Representatives described in section (1).
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to such information being furnished to each party and its directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) (collectively, "Representatives"), each party agrees to treat any nonpublic information concerning to other party (whether prepared by the disclosing party, its advisors or otherwise and irrespective of the form of communication) which is furnished hereunder to a party or to its Representatives now or in the future by or on behalf of the disclosing party (herein collectively referred to as the "Evaluation Material") in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Representatives' to 'Employees' narrows the scope of responsibility. The original text in the contradicted section defines 'Representatives' to include directors, officers, agents, advisors, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers, and financial advisors, in addition to employees. By limiting the responsible party to 'Employees,' the changed text contradicts the broader definition and creates a contradiction because the party is no longer responsible for the breach of agreement by any of its representatives as stated in the contradicted text, but only the employees.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that either party or any of its Representatives have breached this Agreement, then the breaching party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the reasonable legal fees 4 incurred in connection with such litigation, including an appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that either party or any of its Managers have breached this Agreement, then the breaching party shall be liable and pay to the non-breaching party the reasonable legal fees 4 incurred in connection with such litigation, including an appeal therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Representatives' is changed to 'Managers'. This changes the scope of liability for legal fees when there is a breach of contract by replacing the term with 'Managers'. 'Representatives' includes a wide array of people related to each party, including attorneys, accountants and advisors, this is described in section (1). By replacing it with 'Managers', now the legal fees are limited to a breach made only by managers, leading to uncertainty and a contradiction of the initial definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to such information being furnished to each party and its directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) (collectively, "Representatives"), each party agrees to treat any nonpublic information concerning to other party (whether prepared by the disclosing party, its advisors or otherwise and irrespective of the form of communication) which is furnished hereunder to a party or to its Representatives now or in the future by or on behalf of the disclosing party (herein collectively referred to as the "Evaluation Material") in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that if 'either party or any of its Representatives' breaches the agreement, the breaching party is liable for legal fees. The changed text narrows this to 'either party or any of its Managers'. The contradicted text defines 'Representatives' to include a wide array of people, including attorneys, accountants, consultants, etc. By changing 'Representatives' to 'Managers', the changed text creates a contradiction because it limits the scope of liability for legal fees to only breaches committed by managers, excluding other representatives previously included. This creates an in-text contradiction because the definition of Representatives provided in the contradicted text now conflicts with the limited scope implied by the changed text.
